title: Roasted Crispy Energizing Jamaican Basil
description: A flavorful and easy-to-make side dish that combines the freshness of basil with the crunch of roasted vegetables. Perfect for a quick weeknight meal.
instructions: Toss the sweet potatoes and red onion with coconut oil, Jamaican jerk seasoning, salt, and black pepper on a baking sheet.
Roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until the sweet potatoes are tender and lightly browned.
Increase the oven temperature to broil and roast for an additional 5 minutes, or until the red onion is caramelized.
Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat and sauté the minced garlic until fragrant.
Add the chopped fresh basil leaves to the pan and cook for 1-2 minutes, or until wilted.
Remove from heat and stir in lime juice and hot sauce.
To assemble the dish, place the roasted sweet potatoes and red onion on a serving platter and top with the sautéed basil mixture.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve immediately.
